article_title: NextEra Energy in Talks to Acquire Dominion in a Roughly $400 Billion Stock Deal Aimed at AI Data Center Power Demand
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.details:
energyconnects.com: https://www.energyconnects.com/news/utilities/2026/may/nextera-energy-is-said-to-be-in-talks-to-acquire-dominion/

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Content-clean submission with strong cross-source corroboration. Every quantitative claim — $419B enterprise value, $400B headline, $93.36 / $61.73 stock closes, -2.42% / -1.97%, $195B / $54B market caps, 16% / 5.4% YTD, $303B / $111B / $100B / $50B EV/debt splits, 15 GW generation plan, Wells Fargo $66→$68 target, Overweight rating — verified verbatim across multiple snapshots. Both direct quotes (Patterson 'It's a consolidating industry...' and Ketchum's 'all forms of energy' framing) verbatim. The FT/Friday timing, the FERC + DOJ + 'more than a year' approval framing, the Reuters 'AI data centers / industrial reshoring / electrification' demand thesis — all confirmed. Internal cross-reference to 2026-05/12-edgecore-... verified to exist.

source-0.status: Bloomberg relay via EnergyConnects. Verifies: $419B enterprise value, mostly-stock structure, 'Monday' timing, PJM grid context, 16%/5.4% YTD, $195B/$54B caps, NextEra-as-largest-US-utility statement, Patterson/Glenrock 'consolidating industry' quote verbatim, didn't-immediately-respond framing.

source-1.status: Bloomberg Government relay of the FT scoop. Verifies the original FT framing and the no-comment posture from both companies.

source-2.status: Benzinga. Verifies: $93.36/$61.73 closes, -2.42%/-1.97% moves, after-hours direction, Google/Iowa nuclear partnership, '15 gigawatts of new generation capacity over the next decade'.

source-3.status: The Energy Mag. Verifies: Ketchum CEO + 'all forms of energy' pivot (verbatim), $303B NextEra EV, $111B Dominion EV, $100B/$50B debt splits, Carolinas + Florida + Virginia geography.

source-4.status: BOT-BLOCKED (HTTP 403). Article cites this for 'Florida Power & Light, the largest U.S. electric utility'. The underlying claim is verifiable in source-5 (FPL is the regulated electric utility subsidiary) and source-0 (NextEra is largest US utility by market cap). Citation could be improved by re-attributing to an accessible source.

source-5.status: Edgen. Verifies: FERC + DOJ antitrust review framing, 'more than a year' approval timeline, divestiture risk, FPL as regulated electric utility subsidiary.

source-6.status: MarketDash. Confirms $93.36 Friday close, $400B framing — supporting source.

source-7.status: ad-hoc-news.de (German wire) carrying Reuters summary. Verifies: Wells Fargo / Shahriar Pourreza / $66→$68 / Overweight, Reuters attribution of demand thesis (AI data centers + industrial reshoring + electrification).

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Context). Clear sectioning by topic (deal structure, equity, rationale, Ketchum pivot, analyst reaction). Neutral, professional tone. Length appropriate for News (777 words). The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly enumerates open questions (no company confirmation, no exchange ratio, no breakup fees, no regulatory roadmap).
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the reported NextEra-Dominion talks; correctly cross-references the May 12 EdgeCore Northern Virginia article for the data-center-power-demand context.
concerns: Investing.com (source-4) returned HTTP 403 — its citation backs only the 'Florida Power & Light, the largest U.S. electric utility' framing. The underlying fact is supported in source-5 (FPL identity) and source-0 (NextEra-as-largest-US-utility), so the article doesn't have an unsourced claim — but the cited URL itself is unverifiable to the Chief Editor.
recommendations: Re-attribute the 'Florida Power & Light, largest U.S. electric utility' citation to source-5 (Edgen) or source-0 (Bloomberg/EnergyConnects) rather than the bot-blocked Investing.com URL.
Add energyconnects.com, news.bgov.com, benzinga.com, theenergymag.com, edgen.tech, marketdash.com, ad-hoc-news.de to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring financial/M&A coverage outlets.

content_preview.title: NextEra Energy in Talks to Acquire Dominion in a Roughly $400 Billion Stock Deal Aimed at AI Data Center Power Demand
content_preview.summary: FT and Bloomberg report NextEra is in discussions to buy Dominion in a mostly stock deal that would form a $400 billion utility, expanding into PJM and Northern Virginia's data center cluster.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NextEra Energy is in discussions to acquire Dominion Energy in a mostly stock transaction that would create a combined utility with an enterprise value of roughly $400 billion, [according to a Bloomberg report carried by EnergyConnects](https://www.energyconnects.com/news/utilities/2026/may/nextera-energy-is-said-to-be-in-talks-to-acquire-dominion/) and [a Financial Times report relayed by Bloomberg Government](https://news.bgov.com/mergers-and-acquisitions/nextera-energy-is-in-talk...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
